data.body: Mathematician and writer with a focus on the analytical engine. Interested in the marriage of mechanics and abstraction — particularly programs that produce music, art, and proofs.

items.label: Math:
items.value: Differential calculus, Bernoulli numbers, symbolic algebra

items.label: Tools:
items.value: Babbage's difference engine, mechanical loom punched cards

items.title: Translator and Annotator
bullets: Translated Luigi Menabrea's memoir on Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine from French to English.
Authored a set of Notes three times the length of the original, including what is considered the first computer algorithm — a method for computing Bernoulli numbers on the engine.
Anticipated general-purpose computation a century before the first electronic computer.

items.title: Correspondent and Collaborator
bullets: Maintained ongoing technical correspondence with Charles Babbage on the design of the Analytical Engine.
Drafted operational diagrams describing how punched cards could direct the engine through conditional branches and loops.

items.title: Private tutelage in mathematics
items.org: Under Augustus De Morgan, University of London

items.title: Private tutelage in mathematics and music

items.text: Notes on the Analytical Engine, by the Translator (1843)
Letter to Charles Babbage on the Bernoulli sequence (1842)
On the influence of imagination on mathematical reasoning (essay, 1841)
